name: JBSA Randolph
tagline: The 'Showplace of the Air Force': flying training and AETC headquarters, northeast of San Antonio.
_note: JBSA base (San Antonio NE / Universal City). Verified from official sources (jbsa.mil, wilfordhall.tricare.mil, rfisd.net, scucisd.org, randolphfamilyhousing.com, DeCA/AAFES, DoD BAH tool). MHA TX285 (same San Antonio MHA - BAH identical to the other JBSA bases; E-4 directly confirmed at ZIP 78150). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 78148 (Universal City; Schertz/Converse also valid).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (Joint Base San Antonio)
data.Location: Universal City / NE San Antonio, TX
data.Also hosts: HQ Air Education & Training Command + Air Force Personnel Center

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: JBSA-Randolph is home to the 12th Flying Training Wing, which conducts pilot instructor training, combat systems officer training, and other specialized flying training.
It also hosts the headquarters of Air Education and Training Command (AETC), the Air Force's 'First Command' for recruiting, training, and education, plus the Air Force Personnel Center (AFPC).

sections.title: The Showplace of the Air Force

data.subtitle: Randolph is a flying-training base built around one of the military's most iconic landmarks, and a quieter, family-friendly corner of the JBSA cluster.
intro: Its signature is Building 100, the 1931 blue-and-gold-domed administration tower nicknamed the 'Taj Mahal,' now the 12th Flying Training Wing headquarters and the reason Randolph is called the 'Showplace of the Air Force.'
The base sits in the Universal City / Schertz area northeast of San Antonio, so the surrounding communities feel more suburban than the central-city posts.

recommendations.advice: Universal City, Schertz, and Cibolo are the close-in communities, served by Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City ISD.
Randolph Field ISD runs PK-12 on base for military families.
Northeast San Antonio, an easy drive to the rest of JBSA and into the city.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official JBSA-Randolph visitor information page before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-base family housing is privatized, operated by Hunt Military Communities (branded locally as Randolph Family Homes).
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so check the housing office and the partner for current options.

source.label: DoD BAH Rate Lookup (MHA TX285, CY2026)

sections.title: Schools
data: On base, schooling is Randolph Field Independent School District (RFISD), a Texas public district located on JBSA-Randolph serving PK-12 (primarily on-base military families). It is not a DoDEA district.
Off base, the surrounding communities are served largely by Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City ISD (SCUCISD).

data.Clinic: Randolph Clinic (559th Medical Squadron, 59th Medical Wing)
data.Care: Outpatient (inpatient/hospital care at BAMC, Fort Sam Houston)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a neighborhood.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The market is steady and you could rent the home out later if needed.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length and the local market, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on off-base neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining school district or DoDEA early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE region and enrollment so you can be seen at the local military treatment facility or network providers.

data.a: It is the 'Showplace of the Air Force,' a flying-training base with the iconic Taj Mahal tower, home to the 12th Flying Training Wing, HQ AETC, and the Air Force Personnel Center.
Universal City, Schertz, and Cibolo are the close-in communities, served by Schertz-Cibolo-Universal City ISD. On-base housing is run by Hunt (Randolph Family Homes).

data.q: Is it far from the rest of San Antonio?
data.a: It sits northeast of the city, a suburban area with an easy drive to the rest of JBSA and into San Antonio.
MHA TX285 (San Antonio), shared across JBSA. Use the calculator above.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. Tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: The Alamo
picks.blurb: The iconic 1718 mission and battle site in the heart of downtown San Antonio. Free to enter.

picks.name: San Antonio River Walk
picks.blurb: The city's signature network of riverside walkways, restaurants, and boat tours, just below street level downtown.

picks.name: The Pearl
picks.blurb: A former brewery turned dining and shopping district north of downtown, with a popular weekend farmers market.

picks.name: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
picks.blurb: Four Spanish colonial missions preserved by the National Park Service along the river, part of Texas's only UNESCO World Heritage Site.
